title: Fagara coriacea
taxonomy: Fagara coriacea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. The Rutaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus family. Fagara coriacea is further classified as a member of the genus Fagara, which includes several other species of flowering plants. The genus Fagara is part of the Rutaceae family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This classification places Fagara coriacea among other plants with similar characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Fagara coriacea is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues and fever. Its bark, leaves, and roots are often used to prepare medicinal concoctions. Traditionally, Fagara coriacea is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. The plant's extracts are sometimes used to treat skin conditions and respiratory problems. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Fagara coriacea for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Fagara coriacea has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. It is said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the body. Some studies suggest that Fagara coriacea may also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, it may have a role in managing various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Fagara coriacea.
properties: Fagara coriacea is known for its various therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its active compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Furthermore, Fagara coriacea is believed to have antioxidant and analgesic effects, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential therapeutic applications.
constituents: Fagara coriacea is known to contain various active constituents, including limonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents are responsible for the plant's antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, Fagara coriacea has been found to contain flavonoids, glycosides, and other secondary metabolites with potential therapeutic benefits. Research suggests that the active constituents of Fagara coriacea may also exhibit antiparasitic and antiviral properties. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the scope of the plant's bioactive compounds and their applications.
parts:
Fagara coriacea is a plant species in the Rutaceae family. 
Fagara coriacea, also known as the Florida prickly ash, its medicinal parts are primarily its bark and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's properties include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating infections and reducing pain. The bark and roots of Fagara coriacea are rich in bioactive compounds like alkaloids and flavonoids, contributing to its medicinal value. Traditional uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments, but its effectiveness and safety need further scientific evaluation.
preparations: Fagara coriacea is used in traditional medicine, and various herbal preparations have been made from its bark, leaves, and roots. These preparations are often used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In traditional African medicine, Fagara coriacea is used to stimulate digestion and relieve pain. The plant's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential interactions of Fagara coriacea herbal preparations.
side_effects: Fagara coriacea has been traditionally used in folk medicine, but its use is not well-documented in modern medical literature, and its side effects are unknown. However, its close relative, Zanthoxylum, has been reported to have toxic compounds that can cause skin and eye irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal upset. Fagara coriacea, like other members of the Rutaceae family, may cause photosensitivity and other adverse reactions due to the presence of furanocoumarins. Additionally, its use may interact with other medications, leading to unforeseen complications. More research is needed to understand the potential side effects of Fagara coriacea.
intro: Fagara coriacea, also known as Wild Cinnamon or China Pepper, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, characterized by its aromatic leaves and stems. The health benefits of Fagara coriacea include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include limonoids, coumarins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fagara coriacea is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
